topic: Someone finds a wallet containing a large sum of cash
raw_output:
[story 1]

The worn leather of the wallet felt heavy in my palm, the crisp bills spilling out like a sudden, unwelcome flood. My heart gave a strange, tight clench in my chest as I stared at the sheer volume of money. It was more than I could have imagined. I quickly scanned the interior, my fingers trembling slightly as I confirmed the contents. A wave of profound unease washed over me, a dizzying sense of wrongness. I tucked it deep into my coat pocket, the weight a tangible burden. Walking away from where I found it, the pavement seemed too bright, the sounds of the city too loud. I kept my gaze fixed on the ground, trying to shrink into the shadows, wishing the encounter had never happened.

[story 2]

I picked up the object near the bench, the worn fabric of the wallet offering no comfort. When I finally opened it, the sight of the cash made my stomach twist into a knot. My breath hitched, shallow and quick. I felt a sudden, overwhelming urge to disappear. Every movement seemed clumsy, exaggerated by the sheer scale of the find. I slipped the wallet into my bag, the material rubbing against my skin feeling abrasive. I couldn't meet anyone's eyes; the world narrowed down to the frantic beating of my own pulse. I started walking without looking back, each step a heavy, reluctant shuffle, as if dragging an invisible anchor.

[story 3]

The discovery hit me with a sickening thud. My hands shook so violently I almost dropped the leather item. When I finally managed to count the stacks, the numbers swam before my eyes, overwhelming and impossible. A deep, hollow ache settled in my core. I felt an intense pressure behind my eyes, a desperate need to undo what had happened. I carefully placed the wallet down on the nearest surface, as if it were contaminated. I stood there for a long moment, just breathing, the silence amplifying the frantic thumping in my ears. I turned abruptly and walked away, my shoulders slumped forward, the effort of simply existing feeling monumental.
